Campagao Community Trail Loop is a community loop hike in Manduang, Minglanilla. The route follows well-used footpaths through scattered hillside homes, open grasslands, and pockets of vegetation. Rolling climbs and descents, panoramic views of Naga, Minglanilla, and Talisay, and the frequent presence of local communities characterize the documented hike.<\/p>\n\n\n

Trail Snapshot<\/h3>
MILD<\/div>View others \u2192<\/a><\/div>
\n
\ud83d\udcc5 Documented<\/span>July 2026<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udccf Distance<\/span>6.78 km (Loop)<\/strong><\/div>
\u26f0 Elevation Gain<\/span>280 m<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udd52 Duration<\/span>2.5 - 3 hrs<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udca7 Water<\/span>At least 1 liter<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83c\udf33 Shade<\/span>Limited<\/strong><\/div>\n
\ud83d\udc63 Surface<\/span>60% trail, 40% road<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\uddfa\ufe0f GPX<\/span>Supporter Access<\/a><\/strong><\/div> <\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Access<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The documented hike began from a concrete road in Manduang, where motorcycle parking was available along the road near the jump-off point without a parking fee. Public transportation was limited, although habal-habal services were available. The jump-off was also accessible by private vehicle.<\/p>\n\n\n

Why this trail is rated Mild<\/h3>
\u2713 Mostly clear trail with occasional navigation awareness required<\/div>
\u2713 Moderate hiking distance<\/div>
\u2713 Sustained but manageable climbs or descents<\/div>
\u2713 Some isolated sections between communities<\/div>
\u2713 Assistance may require additional time to reach<\/div><\/div>

On this trail:<\/span> cliff<\/a> \u2022<\/span> roads<\/a> \u2022<\/span> stairs<\/a> \u2022<\/span> viewpoint<\/a><\/p><\/div><\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Route Description<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The route follows a well-trodden community trail through scattered hillside homes, open grasslands, and patches of vegetation. Rather than a single sustained ascent, the hike consists of a series of rolling climbs and descents across mostly open terrain with limited shade.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Early sections provide views overlooking Naga, Minglanilla, and Talisay before the trail continues through a quiet rural landscape where houses and small settlements remain present along much of the route. Human activity is evident throughout the hike, although farming activity was limited during this visit.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

The trail to Campagao Peak is well-trodden and easy to follow. The return toward Manduang follows a long concrete staircase. Some sections have moderate cliff exposure, although surrounding vegetation reduces the sense of exposure.<\/p>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n

Trail Conditions<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The trail was dry during the documented hike, which took place on a sunny morning. Shade coverage was limited, and the route was estimated to consist of approximately 60% trail and 40% road.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Mobile signal was available throughout the hike. Stores were present along the route, and the recommended water carry for this hike was at least 1 liter. Most people encountered during the hike were local residents. Insect activity was high during the visit.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Additional Notes<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Increased insect activity was observed throughout the hike.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Although there are isolated stretches between communities, houses and small settlements are present along much of the route, making the trail feel less isolated than many mountain hikes.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Territorial dogs are common along the route. Carrying a hiking stick is recommended.<\/p>\n\n\n\n
